description_long: This sculpture depicts a seated figure with distinct iconography, including a trident (trishula) in one hand and a small animal, likely a bull, at its side. The figure is adorned with a headdress and wears a necklace, with detailed facial features and a serene expression. The posture is cross-legged, and the base is irregular, mimicking a natural rock or platform. The surface has a dark, metallic sheen with some areas showing lighter highlights, suggesting it is cast from bronze or a similar metal. The back of the sculpture is less detailed, focusing on the flowing hair and the continuation of the garment. The overall craftsmanship suggests a representation of a deity, possibly Shiva, commonly found in South Asian religious art.
description: A dark metallic sculpture of a seated deity holding a trident, with a bull at its side and intricate adornments, likely representing Shiva. The figure sits cross-legged on a textured base with detailed facial and ornamental features.
description_view_0: Front view showing the deity's face, trident, necklace, and the bull at the left side, with the cross-legged posture clearly visible.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the profile of the face, the trident, and the bull, with the arm and leg positions more pronounced.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the flowing hair, back of the headdress, and the less detailed back of the figure and base.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the side of the face, the trident, and the bull, with the arm and leg positions from a different angle.
attribute_subject_matter: seated deity figure with trident and bull, likely Shiva
attribute_location_context: No specific installation context is visible; the background is isolated and does not indicate a particular environment.
